patient.chief_complaint: Reynolds pentad (severe): Charcot triad + hypotension + altered mental status — indicates suppurative cholangitis with bacteremic shock
symptoms: Reynolds pentad (severe): Charcot triad + hypotension + altered mental status — indicates suppurative cholangitis with bacteremic shock
Acute onset fever with rigors and chills, often preceded by biliary colic or known cholelithiasis
Rapid clinical deterioration to septic shock if biliary decompression delayed beyond 24–48 hours
signs: Charcot triad: fever/rigors + right upper quadrant pain + jaundice (present together in only 26–72% of cases; each element present in >60%)
Right upper quadrant tenderness on palpation; Murphy sign may be positive (gallbladder co-involvement)
Jaundice with dark urine and pale/clay-colored stools (acholic stool from biliary obstruction)
